observation_basis: Two specimens. Comparison, not pattern. All observations are DESCRIPTIVE — no law-claims.

observations.title: Governance Lifecycle Convergence
observations.observation: Both specimens traverse S0→S1→S2 through the same 5-gate governance lifecycle: CEU reconciliation → proposition derivation → proposition review → revalidation → promotion. The gate sequence, artifact contracts, and operator decision points are identical across specimens. The governance lifecycle is specimen-independent.
observations.divergence: NetBox S0→S1 was by system:governance_projection (structural onboarding). BlueEdge S0→S1 was by operator:khorrix after full gate traversal. BlueEdge's S1→S2 path is explicitly encoded in the SQO execution graph (7 stages); NetBox's was not (pre-dates execution graph). The lifecycle converges; the S1 entry mechanism diverged.

observations.title: Proposition Derivation Divergence
observations.observation: PATH A and PATH B produce dimensionally comparable proposition corpora through fundamentally different evidence channels. PATH A derives from code graph structure (imports, inheritance, centrality). PATH B derives from document evidence structure (CSR domains, SDC capabilities, vault claims, topology edges). Different evidence, comparable yield.
observations.divergence: No class overlap between PATH A and PATH B. This is structurally correct — the proposition classes describe different evidence channels, not different observations of the same evidence. PATH A has higher DIRECT_EVIDENCE ratio (90% vs 62%) because code graph relationships are directly observable. PATH B has more DERIVED because document evidence requires inference across sources.

observations.title: Governance Friction Convergence
observations.observation: Both specimens exhibit genuine governance friction during proposition review. Rejections and arbitrations occur in both — the review process is not rubber-stamping. The friction types differ (code-graph structural disputes vs document-evidence grounding disputes) but the governance mechanism (reject, contest, arbitrate) operates identically.

blueedge.rejection_reason: 3 capabilities in unassigned domains — SDC extraction artifacts, not semantic propositions
blueedge.friction_note: 1 contestation on DOMAIN-17 (weak grounding, 2 DERIVED components) → arbitrated upheld

observations.divergence: NetBox friction was proportionally higher (5 non-accept out of ~77 reviewed = ~6.5%) vs BlueEdge (4 non-accept out of 85 = ~4.7%). NetBox's friction arose from structural coupling disputes; BlueEdge's from evidence grounding gaps. The friction mechanism converges; the friction content diverges with evidence type.

observations.title: Enrichment Mechanism Divergence
observations.observation: Both specimens require enrichment for S2 advancement, but the enrichment mechanisms are fundamentally different. PATH A enriches through code graph authority edges (AST analysis, import/inheritance topology). PATH B enriches through HTML document structure re-extraction (domain ID correction via semantic name matching, component count recalculation). Both strengthen weak propositions but through different evidence sources.

netbox.enrichment_source: AST analysis of Python codebase (3,614 edges)
netbox.mechanism: Import/inheritance topology enriches structural dominance and coupling claims

blueedge.mechanism: Semantic name matching corrects domain ID mismatch between canonical CSR and SDC candidate CSR
blueedge.limitation: No code graph available. Cannot provide L5 structural authority from document evidence alone.

observations.divergence: PATH A enrichment adds structural depth (authority edges). PATH B enrichment corrects structural alignment (domain ID mapping). PATH A can reach L5 authority through code-level proof. PATH B is ceiling-limited at L3 because document evidence cannot provide code-level structural authority. The enrichment contract converges; the evidence ceiling diverges.

observations.title: Revalidation Pattern Convergence
observations.observation: The same 25-check deterministic revalidation engine runs on both specimens without modification. Both achieve PASS (25/25). The revalidation contract (8 phases: input gate, proposition integrity, confidence realism, tier validity, disposition completeness, reconciliation cleanliness, spine consistency, SQO state consistency) transfers across specimen types.

blueedge.note: Same engine, no PATH-specific adaptation required

observations.divergence: NetBox's earlier revalidation used a 48-check/9-phase framework. The current 25-check/8-phase engine was written during BlueEdge lifecycle exercise and retroactively applies to both. No divergence in the current engine's behavior across specimens.

observations.title: Constitutional Anchor Behavior
observations.observation: The constitutional replay anchor compares candidate corpus against reference (NetBox) across 8 dimensions. BlueEdge achieved 8/8 PASS at S2 (CONSTITUTIONAL_DISTANCE_ACCEPTABLE). The anchor correctly detected dimensional gaps at S1 (enrichment_activity: FAIL, review_obligations: FAIL) and cleared them after enrichment. The anchor operates as a specimen-independent semantic adequacy check.
observations.divergence: The anchor uses NetBox as the fixed reference point. This means the anchor measures conformity to PATH A dimensional norms, not universal governance norms. A future third specimen might expose dimensions where NetBox is atypical. The anchor is currently a single-reference comparator, not a multi-specimen norm.

observations.title: S2 Advancement Legitimacy
observations.observation: Both specimens hold S2 through GOVERNED_LIFECYCLE provenance, but arrived there through different mechanisms. NetBox's S2 was the first governed advancement; BlueEdge's S2 was earned through the SQO execution graph's explicit S1→S2 path (7 stages). Both S2 states represent operator-authorized advancement after revalidation PASS, but BlueEdge's is structurally more rigorous because it was governed by an encoded execution graph rather than ad-hoc process.

netbox.execution_graph: Pre-dates SQO execution graph. Process was correct but not graph-encoded.
netbox.constitutional_anchor: Not integrated at time of S2 advancement

blueedge.execution_graph: SQO execution graph v2.0.0 — 7-stage S1→S2 path
blueedge.constitutional_anchor: Integrated — 8/8 PASS required and verified before advancement

observations.divergence: NetBox achieved S2 before the constitutional anchor and SQO execution graph existed. BlueEdge achieved S2 with both in place. BlueEdge's S2 is therefore more structurally constrained — it passed through explicit gates that NetBox did not face. This is not a deficiency in NetBox — it reflects governance infrastructure maturation between specimens.

observations.title: Debt Taxonomy Convergence
observations.observation: Both specimens use the same debt classification taxonomy: IRREDUCIBLE_STRUCTURAL_ABSENCE, REDUCED_BY_ENRICHMENT, REDUCIBLE_BY_EVIDENCE. The taxonomy transfers across specimen types. Debt items in both specimens are classified against the same structural authority requirements.
observations.divergence: BlueEdge has more debt items (15 vs NetBox's smaller set) because PATH B evidence cannot provide L5 structural authority — document evidence reaches a ceiling that code graph evidence does not. The taxonomy is the same; the distribution reflects evidence channel limitations.

observations.title: Authority Ceiling Convergence
observations.observation: Both specimens operate at L3 authority ceiling for AI-derived propositions. The non-automatable boundary holds across specimen types. No proposition in either specimen was auto-promoted — all governance transitions required operator action.

netbox.operator_actions: review_accept, review_reject, review_contest, escalate_arbitration, resolve_arbitration, s2_advancement_granted

blueedge.operator_actions: confirm, accept, reject, contest, arbitrate, accept-unflagged, complete, advance

observations.divergence: BlueEdge exercised more operator action types (8 vs 6) because it traversed Gate 1 (CEU reconciliation) as an explicit operator gate. The L3 ceiling is identical. The non-automatable boundary is structural — it is enforced by the SQO execution graph, not by specimen type.

summary.interpretation: Two specimens completing the same governance lifecycle through different evidence channels is a comparison, not a pattern. The governance infrastructure (gates, revalidation, anchor, promotion) converges. The evidence substrate (code graph vs document evidence) diverges. Diversity within common governance is itself an observation — not a law.
